text:
We have two ropes, each takes 60 minutes to burn from end to end, but they burn unevenly.  
To measure 45 minutes:

1. Light one rope at both ends and the second rope at one end simultaneously.  
2. The first rope (burning from both ends) will be completely burnt in 30 minutes.  
3. At that moment, the second rope has 30 minutes of burn time left (since it was burning from one end for 30 minutes).  
4. Immediately light the second end of the second rope. It will now burn from both ends, taking 15 minutes to finish.  
5. Total time from start: 30 + 15 = 45 minutes.

This uses both ropes, so the minimum number of ropes used is 2.

Final integer: 2
